business.headline: India-US partnership driven by trust and shared economic growth: Piyush GoyalSpeaking at the Annual Leadership Summit of the American Chamber of Commerce in India in New Delhi, Goyal said India has become a trusted partner for global businesses and is well-positioned to benefit from changing global economic trends.

AI saves 75% of my time: Anil Agarwal on why Indians should not fear new techWhat if one tool could save hours of your working day? According to Vedanta Chairman Anil Agarwal, technology is already doing exactly that. Sharing his own experience, he said it saves 75% of his time and urged Indians, especially youngsters, to embrace it rather than fear it.

Samsung chip workers could get bonuses worth nearly $400,000 amid AI boom: ReportUnder the proposed plan, bonuses will be closely linked to how well each business unit performs. Since Samsung's memory-chip business is currently benefiting from booming demand for AI memory products, employees in that segment stand to gain the most.
Sensex, Nifty erase early gains as RBI rate hike fears weigh on marketsThe BSE Sensex closed 135.03 points lower at 75,183.36, while the NSE Nifty50 slipped 4.30 points to end at 23,789.00.

Why the rupee's fall could make owning a home more expensive for youThe rupee's sharp slide is raising concerns that the RBI may need to raise rates. That could lift home loan EMIs and make buying a house harder for borrowers.

Rupee's fall sparks concern. But is the whole of Asia under pressure too?The rupee has extended its losing streak as a stronger dollar, costly crude and foreign outflows weigh on it. The sharper slide against several Asian peers has turned the move into a wider inflation and trade concern.

Goods transport costs rise from today, daily essentials may soon get costlierTransporters have introduced a fuel-linked surcharge as diesel prices climb. The move could push up logistics costs for groceries, medicines and online deliveries.

RBI announces $5 billion swap auction amid rupee weaknessIn a circular, the RBI said it had decided to conduct a USD 5 billion USD/INR buy-sell swap auction after reviewing current and changing liquidity conditions.

Silver slips by more than Rs 4,000: 3 key reasons behind the sharp fallIf you have been tracking silver prices, Thursday may have come as a surprise. Silver dropped by over Rs 4,000 on the MCX, even as global uncertainty remained high. Here are the three key factors behind the sharp fall.

India's power demand hits fresh record for 3rd straight day amid heatwaveThe country's peak power demand touched 265.44 gigawatts (GW) at 3:45 pm on Wednesday, going past Tuesday's previous high of 260.45 GW, according to data shared by the Ministry of Power.

Could the rupee ever hit 150 against the US dollar?Most economists will still rule out the viral prediction about the rupee hitting 150 against the US dollar. But in a world shaped by wars, oil shocks and volatile capital flows, is the debate really that absurd anymore?

Nvidia cashes in on AI gold rush with record earnings and $80 billion buybackNvidia Q1 results: The chip giant reported another blockbuster quarter, posting record revenue and profit numbers as global technology companies continue spending heavily on AI infrastructure, data centres and advanced computing systems.

'Melody' buzz keeps fuelling the wrong Parle on D-Street as stock jumps 5% againParle Industries shares hit an upper circuit again after videos showed Prime Minister Narendra Modi gifting Melody toffees to Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni. The move drew attention because the stock has no link to Parle Products, pointing to mistaken-identity buying.

Sensex, Nifty open higher as crude oil eases on hopes of Iran peace dealThe BSE Sensex jumped 345.02 points, or 0.46%, to 75,663.41 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 rose 131.30 points, or 0.55%, to 23,790.30 as of 9:44 am.

Why have cab and auto drivers called a 3-day strike in Delhi-NCRMore than 68 transport unions have begun a three-day strike across Delhi-NCR, affecting cabs and autos. Drivers say rising fuel costs, stagnant fares and app commissions have pushed them under severe financial strain.

Silent listings? Why companies are going for confidential IPO filingsMore Indian companies are turning to Sebi's confidential IPO filing route as West Asia tensions unsettle markets. The option gives them more time and less public exposure before deciding when to launch.

James Murdoch to buy New York Magazine and Vox podcasts in $300 million dealJames Murdoch's Lupa Systems is acquiring New York Magazine, Vox's podcast network and Vox.com for more than $300 million. The deal adds a major magazine brand and sought-after podcast talent to his growing media portfolio.

Gita Gopinath says jobs, inflation bigger worry for India than rupee at 100As the West Asia conflict drags on, Gita Gopinath warns India may face sharper fuel hikes, rising inflation and a weaker rupee, while arguing the country still has enough economic strength to avoid panic.

Gita Gopinath discusses impact of West Asia conflict, rising oil prices and moreIn an exclusive interview with India Today, Gita Gopinath, Professor of Economics at Harvard University, discusses the impact of the West Asia conflict and rising oil prices on the Indian economy.

Sebi plans PF, NPS-like payroll-linked MF SIPs. Details hereInvesting in mutual funds through salary deductions, much like PF and NPS, may soon become possible. SEBI has proposed a new framework that could also allow donations through mutual fund units.

entertainment.headline: Varun Dhawan's Hai Jawani… trailer launch cancelled over technical glitch The trailer launch of Varun Dhawan's Hai Jawani Toh Ishq Hona Hai was cancelled at the last minute. The makers cited a technical glitch, while sources suggested the move was linked to a legal dispute over material involving Biwi No. 1.

Mohanlal celebrates birthday, cuts cake with fans on Drishyam 3 release day Mohanlal celebrated his 66th birthday at a Drishyam 3 screening after the film opened in theatres. Viral videos show the actor cutting a cake and waving at fans after the premiere.

Saba Azad on Hrithik Roshan's Storm, delay in Anurag Kashyap's Bandar release Actor Saba Azad opens up about the release of Anurag Kashyap's Bandar, working with Bobby Deol and Sanya Malhotra, and how Who's Your Gynac? sparked conversations around taboo topics.

Kim Soo-hyun cleared of allegations of dating Kim Sae-ron as a minor: Report South Korean actor Kim Soo-hyun has reportedly been cleared of underage dating allegations, while prosecutors now seek a YouTuber's arrest in a dramatic new twist to the controversy.

We all have pasts: Tejasswi defends fiance Karan Kundrra amid cheating rumours Tejasswi Prakash opened up about marriage rumours surrounding her relationship with Karan Kundrra and addressed cheating allegations linked to his past during their appearance on Desi Bling. The episode also featured Karan proposing to Tejasswi after more than four years together.

Zeenat Aman calls herself a comeback queen, opens up on painful marriage phase Zeenat Aman said Gawaahi marked her return to films as her marriage to Mazhar Khan was breaking down. She said work helped her escape a painful personal phase and reclaim her identity beyond family roles.

Mouni Roy makes Cannes red carpet debut, friend Disha Patani cheers her on Actor Mouni Roy made her Cannes red carpet debut in a white gown. Her appearance drew attention online, with her friend Disha Patani praising her look on social media.

The Boys ending explained: Why finale works even when fans cannot agree on it The Boys finale has divided the internet, but its messy, emotional ending stays true to the show's dark satire, brutal morality and chaotic storytelling.

Only fair kids: Actor Ulka Gupta recalls battling colourism in industry as child Actor Ulka Gupta, who has been in the industry for nearly two decades, spoke about facing colourism and repeated rejection as a child artiste before she made her debut with the 2006 show, Resham Dankh. In an exclusive interview, the actor highlighted how skin-tone bias shaped casting decisions and self-worth in television.

Once rejected, Ayushmann Khurrana returns to Indian Idol as star. Video Ayushmann Khurrana returned to the Indian Idol stage and performed Dil Waale Chor from Pati Aur Woh Do during a special episode featuring Helen. The appearance marked a full-circle moment for the actor, who was once rejected on the show.

Chiranjeevi reunites with director Bobby Kolli, Pawan Kalyan attends launch Chiranjeevi launched his 158th film with director Bobby Kolli at the mahurat ceremony. The launch on Thursday was attended by brothers Pawan Kalyan and Nagababu.

Shirt utaarega maamu? The Salman Khan moment that changed Bobby Deol's career Actor Bobby Deol recalled how Salman Khan's offer for Race 3 came at a time when he felt the younger generation had forgotten him. The film became a turning point in his career and helped him reconnect with audiences.

Dragon director Prashanth Neel decodes Jr NTR's intense 15 kg weight loss for film Actor Jr NTR lost 15 kilos in four months for Dragon. Director Prashanth Neel has now revealed how the actor's intense transformation worried his family and team.

Dragon beyond Jr NTR: Meet the other monsters from Prashanth Neel's world Jr NTR may lead Dragon as the ruthless Luger, but the film's explosive supporting cast could steal the spotlight just as easily. Here's why Prashanth Neel's dark opium-war world looks stacked with scene-stealers.

Prime Video, Sony LIV, ZEE5 this week: System, Satrangi Badle Ka Khel and more New OTT releases on May 22 bring a mix of courtroom drama, romance, revenge, and mystery across Prime Video, Sony LIV, and ZEE5. Titles like System, Madhuvidhu, Satrangi, and Memu Copulam headline the week's fresh streaming lineup.

Drishyam 3 review: Mohanlal-Jeethu Joseph's sequel is less clever, more dramaticRelease Date:Thu May 21, 2026 Drishyam 3 movie review: Director Jeethu Joseph's Drishyam 3, starring Mohanlal, Meena and Siddique, is a sequel that prioritises drama over intelligence. While the film has some meaty ideas that could make for a brilliant thriller, it settles for less because of its writing.

Netflix this week: Dhurandhar Raw and Undekha, Ladies First and more Netflix is set to release a diverse slate of titles this week, including the uncut version of Dhurandhar, the sci-fi drama The Boroughs, the satirical film Ladies First and Bad Thoughts Season 2.

Saswata Chatterjee to play Sourav Ganguly's father Chandi Ganguly in biopic Saswata Chatterjee has begun filming in Mumbai as Sourav Ganguly's father in the cricketer's biopic. The shoot has now moved into production with key family roles cast and the Kolkata schedule due this month.

Anurag Kashyap, Dia Mirza follow viral Cockroach Janta Party on Instagram From Anurag Kashyap to Dia Mirza, several celebrities have joined the online buzz around the viral Cockroach Janta Party. Here's why the satirical movement is taking over social media.

general news.headline: NTA chief denies NEET-UG 2026 paper leak, says only certain questions were outNTA officials told the Parliamentary Standing Committee that the NEET UG 2026 examination paper was not leaked in full and only certain questions from the paper had surfaced before the exam.

Cow sacrifice not part of Eid: Calcutta High Court upholds ban on cattle slaughterCalcutta High Court upholds the West Bengal government's May 13 notification restricting slaughter of bulls, cows and buffaloes ahead of Eid al-Adha and directed the state to clarify that cow sacrifice is not an essential religious practice under Islam.

Pulwama attack mastermind, Hamza Burhan, killed by unknown men in POKHamza Burhan, known as 'Doctor', sustained multiple bullet injuries after he was attacked in Muzaffarabad. Burhan, a J&K native, was one of the masterminds of the 2019 Pulwama attack, which left 40 CRPF personnel dead.

Cockroach Party creates fresh X account hours after block; taunts critics in postThe Cockroach Janta Party made a comeback on X with a new account just hours after its original handle was withheld in India, amid its rapidly growing social media popularity. The satirical outfit has recently surpassed the BJP's Instagram follower count, emerging as a major youth-driven political satire movement online.

The cockroach that just won't go awayThe Cockroach Janta Party overtook BJP's Instagram followers' count, was blocked on X, and then came back. A lot happened throughout the day with the viral, satirical sensation that refuses to fade from public attention. Here's a recap.

Drishyam 3 review: Mohanlal-Jeethu Joseph's sequel is less clever, more dramaticRelease Date:Thu May 21, 2026Drishyam 3 movie review: Director Jeethu Joseph's Drishyam 3, starring Mohanlal, Meena and Siddique, is a sequel that prioritises drama over intelligence. While the film has some meaty ideas that could make for a brilliant thriller, it settles for less because of its writing.

Twisha Sharma death case: Seven questions that continue to haunt probeQuestions over CCTV footage, evidence handling and call records have sharpened scrutiny of Twisha Sharma's death in Bhopal. With her family alleging foul play and refusing cremation, the mystery has only deepened.

Meta delayed layoffs to automate jobs? Viral post reveals employees were given a special taskA viral X post has claimed that Meta employees were asked to build internal AI tools months before fresh layoffs began. The post has reignited concerns over whether AI is slowly replacing white-collar jobs inside big tech firms.

Collapsed Bihar bridge gets a bridge to bridge itBihar authorities are building temporary Bailey bridges over the collapsed part of the Vikramshila Setu in Bhagalpur. The Bailey bridges are expected to restore limited movement from June 5 after safety checks and completion of the remaining structures.

Rs 20 lakh on IIT dream, Rs 10 lakh on fees, and placements that don't pay backParents are emptying savings on Rs 5–8 lakh in coaching and Rs 8–10 lakh as IIT fees, only to watch "average" offers flirt with Rs 26 LPA (IIT Bombay's recent placement report), before tax, before rent, before EMIs. If this is India's gold standard, why does this math look like a liability, not a launchpad?

Humayun Kabir, TMC MLA use Eid qurbani in first direct challenge to BJP in BengalIn the first major challenge to the Suvendu Adhikari-led West Bengal government, AJUP MLA Humayun Kabir has vowed to continue the 1,400-year-old tradition of Eid qurbani despite cow slaughter restrictions. TMC MLA Akhruzzaman moved the Calcutta High Court challenging the curbs. However, several Muslim leaders urged the community to refrain from cow sacrifice this year.

Hundreds face heat, uncertainty as bulldozers roll through Mumbai's Garib NagarHundreds of residents in Mumbai's Garib Nagar have been left homeless after Western Railway launched a demolition drive on Tuesday to clear illegal structures near Bandra East railway station, with many residents claiming they were given only 24 hours to retrieve their belongings.

Found in Mumbai drain: Items for a fully furnished house, autorickshaw, police barricadeMumbai's pre-monsoon drain cleaning has pulled out household furniture, an autorickshaw and even a police barricade from eastern suburban nullahs this year. The recoveries show how Mumbaikars have turned the drains into landfills.

Friday forecast: Blistering heat in north, thunderstorms in northeastAs the heat grip over north and central India refuses to loosen, most of the country saw virtually no rain on Thursday. Friday brings a different story for some regions.

Cockroach party beats BJP on the 'gram with 10 million followers in 5 daysA satirical political campaign, the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP), born online after the Chief Justice of India's remarks on unemployed youth, has crossed 10 million Instagram followers in five days. The Abhijeet Dipke-founded CJP has managed to surpass the ruling BJP's follower count.

Actor, who worked with Bachchan and Aamir Khan, arrested after 12 years on the runHemant Modi, 53, was lodged in Mehsana jail before being granted parole by the Gujarat High Court in 2014. He was supposed to surrender to the authorities after the completion of a 30-day parole period, but he failed to do that, and was officially declared a 'parole jumper' on July 25, 2014.

Can cockroaches survive nuclear war? The truth behind the viral mythCockroaches are often called the only creatures that could survive nuclear war. The truth is more complicated. While they can tolerate radiation better than humans, they are far from indestructible. Here is how science, Cold War fears, and evolutionary survival turned cockroaches into the world's most persistent apocalypse myth.

Can the Cockroach Janta Party get a cockroach poll symbol?The Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) wants a mobile phone as its election symbol, but that's not on the Election Commission's free symbols list. Can the CJP use cockroach as its poll symbol? Here's a look at ECI's poll-symbols list and the rules.

CBSE rechecking chaos: Blurry copies, payment glitches, portal crashes spark outrageCBSE's re-evaluation nightmare has stretched into a third straight day, with Class 12 students and parents battling crashing portals, failed payments, unreadable scanned copies and growing fears over evaluation quality. Amid JEE, NEET and CUET pressure, many say CBSE's rechecking and post-result process has turned into a mentally exhausting mess.
Rain coming? Satellite shows new western disturbance approaching IndiaFresh IMD satellite images show a strong western disturbance moving towards north India amid a severe heatwave.

Melody is chocolatey. But how much chocolate does it have?The Narendra Modi-Giorgia Meloni Melody moment has put the candy being produced by Parle since the 1980s into the global spotlight. Parle has been marketing Melody as a chocolatey candy. But how much cocoa does Parle's Melody exactly have?

World Cup: Germany recall Manuel Neuer, 40, as first-choice goalkeeperGermany coach Julian Nagelsmann has brought veteran goalkeeper Manuel Neuer out of international retirement and reinstated him as the country's first-choice goalkeeper for the upcoming World Cup. The decision marks a major shift in Germany's plans after Oliver Baumann had been backed as the No. 1 following Euro 2024.

As Cockroach Janta Party's X account is blocked, 2 trademark claims emergeTwo trademark applications have been filed for "Cockroach Janta Party," the satirical political outfit that emerged after Chief Justice of India Surya Kant's "cockroach" remarks during a Supreme Court hearing.

Chief Minister Vijay's week of rollbacksVijay's opening week as Tamil Nadu Chief Minister has been marked by controversies and quick rollbacks of government orders. It remains to be seen whether these are merely first-week jitters or signs of a newbie TVK government struggling to balance optics, governance and coalition pangs.

sports.headline: East Bengal end 22-year wait for major trophy, Mohun Bagan suffer front-row pain East Bengal end 22-year wait for major Indian trophy with their maiden Indian Supr League title. Mohun Bagan saw their title defence hopes slip away on thrilling final day.

After Kohli's fitness remark, Manpreet invites RCB star for training challenge Manpreet Singh has invited Virat Kohli and the RCB squad for a joint training session and yo-yo test. The exchange followed Kohli's admission that Indian cricketers are nowhere near hockey players in terms of fitness.

Rajat Patidar back in time for RCB's top-two push vs SRH, confirms Mo Bobat IPL 2026: Mo Bobat confirmed Rajat Patidar is fit again and training before RCB's match against Sunrisers Hyderabad. His return sharpens Bengaluru's push for a top-two finish as Bobat also highlighted Bhuvneshwar Kumar's value across the dressing room.

National anti-doping act to be amended, jail term proposed for drug suppliers The Centre has proposed amending the National Anti-Doping Act to criminalise the supply and trafficking of banned substances to athletes. The move hardens India's anti-doping stance as its poor WADA record collides with major hosting ambitions.

Rohit Sharma, Virat Kohli doing everything to last till 2027 World Cup: Ricky Ponting Ricky Ponting believes Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma are still pushing hard towards one final ODI World Cup run in 2027. While Kohli continues dominating with the bat, Rohit's biggest challenge may now be whether his body can keep up with another two-year cycle.

We are Champions daddy: How Arteta learnt Arsenal ended 22-year EPL wait Arsenal sealed the Premier League title after Manchester City drew with Bournemouth. The moment ended a 22-year wait and allowed Arsenal to shift attention towards the Champions League final.

Afghanistan women's refugee team to tour England under ECB initiative The (ECB) will host an Afghanistan Refugee Women's Team for a tour beginning on June 22 under a special initiative aimed at supporting displaced Afghan cricketers and promoting inclusivity in the sport.

Why drop Hosein? CSK fans left fuming as spinner dropped for Short vs GT Chennai Super Kings left out Akeal Hosein and brought in Matt Short for a must-win game against Gujarat Titans. The call jarred with supporters because Hosein had been one of CSK's more effective bowlers while the playoff equation remained unforgiving.

World Cup: Germany recall Manuel Neuer, 40, as first-choice goalkeeper Germany coach Julian Nagelsmann has brought veteran goalkeeper Manuel Neuer out of international retirement and reinstated him as the country's first-choice goalkeeper for the upcoming World Cup. The decision marks a major shift in Germany's plans after Oliver Baumann had been backed as the No. 1 following Euro 2024.

French Open 2026 draw: Jannik Sinner, Novak Djokovic learn about path to title Jannik Sinner and Novak Djokovic have landed on opposite sides of the French Open 2026 draw. That keeps open the prospect of a final between the top seed chasing a Career Grand Slam and Djokovic hunting a 25th major.

KKR star added to India A squad for Sri Lanka tri-series, replaces Harsh DubeyKKR star Anukul Roy has been drafted into the India A squad for the Sri Lanka tri-series in place of Harsh Dubey. The move rewards his strong KKR season as Anukul has been starter for Kolkata this season.

Nabi vs Brar: Why was one of India's best pacers not given Test debut?Jammu and Kashmir pacer Auqib Nabi guided his team to a historic Ranji Trophy victory this season. The incredible red-ball performance got him a white-ball IPL contract, but was not deemed good enough for the Indian Test team.

Novak Djokovic announces new head coach ahead of French Open: Who is Viktor Troicki?Novak Djokovic has appointed Viktor Troicki as his new head coach before the French Open. The move reunites two long-time Serbian allies as he chases a record 25th Grand Slam title.

Hardik Pandya sanctioned for misconduct during Mumbai Indians' loss to KKRHardik Pandya was fined 10 per cent of his match fee and received one demerit point after the Mumbai Indians captain forcefully dislodged the bails during the IPL 2026 match against Kolkata Knight Riders at the Eden Gardens on Wednesday.

Playing through pain: Varun refuses to take the easy option, says Shane WatsonKKR spinner Varun Chakravarthy should ideally be resting but simply refuses to give up on the team's campaign, said assistant coach Shane Watson. KKR have made an incredible comeback in the second half of the season after remaining winless for the first 6 games this season.

Beer, champagne and cigarettes? 1983 World Cup winner debunks viral party billFormer India cricketer Kirti Azad dismissed the viral restaurant bill linked to India's 1983 World Cup celebrations as fake, asserting that the team never visited the hotel mentioned in the receipt and that Kapil Dev's signature on the document had been forged.

East Bengal, Mohun Bagan headline four-way ISL title battle on dramatic final dayEast Bengal and Mohun Bagan headline a dramatic final day in the ISL title race, but Punjab FC and Mumbai City FC are still very much alive too. With four clubs separated by the finest margins, Thursday night could swing wildly with every goal across Kolkata and New Delhi.

Virat Kohli, Rohit Sharma not automatic choices for 2027 ODI World Cup: SourcesAjit Agarkar and his selection panel deliberated resting Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma for the upcoming three-match ODI series. With the ODI World Cup more than a year away, selection will be based on consistent performances, sources said.

GT vs CSK preview: Can Chennai keep their stuttering hopes alive in Ahmedabad?IPL 2026: Destiny isn't in the hands of CSK as they look to keep their fading playoff hopes alive in Ahmedabad against GT. Gujarat will be aiming to get back to winning ways and tighten up their performance in front of their home crowd.

Cricket Canada controversy: Gunshots fired at board president's houseThe controversy surrounding Cricket Canada took a shocking turn after gunshots were fired at the house of newly elected board president Arvinder Khosa. The incident comes amid ongoing investigations into alleged match-fixing, corruption and suspected links between figures associated with Canadian cricket and the Lawrence Bishnoi gang.

All roads lead to 'Villa' for Europa League king Unai EmeryAston Villa won their first Europa League title on Wednesday, May 20, under the coaching of Unai Emery. This was the Spanish coach's fifth Europa trophy, funnily with clubs having 'Villa' in their name.

No trip to Ahmedabad? MS Dhoni heads to Ranchi on CSK's final matchdayIPL 2026: MS Dhoni left for Ranchi on CSK's final league matchday after suffering a thumb injury. Chennai Super Kings will take on Gujarat Titans in their final league game with the play-offs hopes hanging by a thread.

The forgotten IPL OG: How cricket repaid Manish Pandey in KolkataIPL 2026: He has played all 19 IPL seasons, batted just 28 times in six years, and never stopped showing up. On a tricky Eden Gardens pitch, with KKR's playoff dream on the line, Manish Pandey finally got his night.

IPL Play of the Day: Sunil Narine weaves his magic again to keep Kolkata afloatIPL 2026, KKR vs MI: Sunil Narine produced another outstanding performance for Kolkata Knight Riders on Wednesday as the three-time champions kept their playoff hopes alive with a four-wicket victory over Mumbai Indians at the Eden Gardens.

Hardik Pandya rues fielding collapse behind MI's disastrous IPL 2026 seasonIPL 2026: Hardik Pandya admitted Mumbai Indians' poor fielding standards became one of the biggest reasons behind their disastrous IPL 2026 campaign. After another sloppy performance against KKR, MI's season now heads towards an underwhelming finish with serious questions looming over the franchise.

Manish Pandey repays KKR faith with match-winning MI knock: That's why I'm hereIPL 2026: Manish Pandey finally got his first real batting opportunity of IPL 2026 and instantly turned back the clock for KKR. On a tense Eden Gardens night, the veteran repaid Kolkata's faith with a vintage chase-masterclass that kept their playoff hopes alive.

IPL playoffs scenario: How Kolkata Knight Riders can still seal 4th spotIPL 2026, KKR vs MI: Kolkata Knight Riders have given themselves a great chance of qualifying for the playoffs after beating the Mumbai Indians by four wickets on Wednesday at the Eden Gardens in Kolkata.

Who came in for Angkrish Raghuvanshi vs MI? KKR's concussion sub sparks debateIPL 2026: KKR's handling of Angkrish Raghuvanshi's substitution during the win over Mumbai Indians triggered confusion. The lack of clarity over a reported concussion replacement turned the match into a rules debate.

KKR vs MI: A Manish Pandey classic at the Eden keeps Kolkata in playoff raceManish Pandey steadied Kolkata Knight Riders after an early wobble and carried the chase against Mumbai Indians to help them remain in the hunt for the IPL 2026 playoffs. MI's batting woes continued with the bat, and they struggled with their catching as Mumbai lost their ninth match of the season.

Couple goals: Gael Monfils, Elina Svitolina train together at French OpenTennis power couple Gael Monfils and Elina Svitolina thrilled fans after training together at Roland Garros, with their practice session quickly going viral on social media. Fresh off her Italian Open triumph, Svitolina heads into the French Open in top form, while Monfils is set to enjoy massive home support in Paris.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
